fields.name: Street Thug
fields.description: Nobody's soldier. Works alone or in loose pairs, opportunistic. Not trained, not disciplined, but willing enough that it amounts to the same thing in a dark stairwell.

fields.name: Two Street Thugs
fields.description: Separately, a problem. Together, a system â€” one pushes, one flanks. They've done it before. Not coordinated exactly, but practiced.

fields.name: Three Street Thugs
fields.description: Numbers are the whole strategy. They know it. You know it. The only question is whether you can make that strategy expensive enough that someone reconsiders.

fields.name: Corner Boy
fields.description: Kings or BSB, depending on the block. Low-tier, more committed than a generic thug. Has something to prove, which makes him more dangerous than his experience warrants.

fields.name: Westside Runner
fields.description: Young, fast, not yet sure how much he's willing to lose. Kings material but not there yet. The danger is that he's trying to prove something, which makes him harder to read than someone who knows what they're doing.

fields.name: Two Westside Runners
fields.description: Both young, both fast, both with something to prove. The problem with fighting people who haven't learned their limits yet is that they don't know when to stop.

fields.name: Terrell
fields.description: Young, maybe twenty-five. Gold chain, clean sneakers, eyes that clock you the second you round the corner. Got reach but no discipline. The stoop light flickers. Somewhere above you a window slides shut.

fields.name: Harlan Street Muscle
fields.description: Dutch sends muscle, not lawyers. This is the muscle. Older than the Kings' guys, quieter about it, less interested in the theatre of the thing. Has done this enough times that it's just work.

fields.name: Two Harlan Muscle
fields.description: One talks, one doesn't. The one who doesn't is the problem. Dutch's people work in pairs for a reason â€” they've thought about the geometry of a room before they walk into it.

fields.name: Harlan Enforcer
fields.description: Mid-tier. Professional. Doesn't get creative, doesn't make mistakes. Not the guy Dutch sends when he wants to make a point â€” the guy Dutch sends when the point has already been made and ignored.

fields.name: Dutch's Enforcer
fields.description: Doesn't talk much. Doesn't need to. The fact that Dutch sent him specifically means you've cleared through everything else and arrived somewhere serious.

fields.name: Kings Soldier
fields.description: Generational Westside. Knows every block, every exit, every face that doesn't belong. Fights like someone who grew up fighting â€” territorial, committed, not easily rattled.

fields.name: Kings Enforcer
fields.description: Senior muscle. Deliberate. Slower than a soldier but reads the room in a way the soldiers don't bother with. The loose council structure means he's answering to someone, but you won't know who until it matters.

fields.name: Kings Lieutenant
fields.description: One of several. The internal politics mean he's always half-managing someone else's problem as well as his own. Experienced, genuinely dangerous, and irritable about it.

fields.name: Pruitt's Crew
fields.description: Loyalists. Fight together and won't run unless Pruitt's out. The northern Westside blocks are their floor and they know every inch of it. Removing them is a statement, and the Kings' council will read it as one.

fields.name: BSB Soldier
fields.description: Punches up in self-image. Punches at or below weight in practice. Craig's people confuse flash for power and it shows. The danger is volume â€” they come in numbers because individually they know better.

fields.name: Darius
fields.description: Named enemy. Competent. Mid-level operator in Craig's orbit who runs Strip-adjacent work without permission. Young, ambitious, committed to a version of himself that is doing very well. Loses because he misjudged the situation, not because he's weak. Dangerous in a rematch.

fields.name: Craig's Security
fields.description: Professional hired help, not crew. Impersonal. More effective than BSB regulars because they don't have anything to prove â€” they're just working. Craig pays well enough to get the good kind.

fields.name: Bucco Associate
fields.description: Old world structure means old world patience. He will wait for the right moment in a way that street crews genuinely can't. Suit jacket. Doesn't raise his voice. The calm is the threat.

fields.name: Bucco Muscle
fields.description: Hired. Large. The kind of large that comes with a suit. Very little expression. Doesn't want to be here any more than you do, which is somehow not reassuring.

fields.name: Bucco Capo
fields.description: Been with the family long enough that Richie Bucco knows his children's names. That's not warmth â€” that's depth of investment. Removing him is a family matter now, not just a business one.

fields.name: Hostile Dockworker
fields.description: Not mob, but mob-adjacent. Union solidarity takes interesting forms at the Docks. Has been doing physical work for thirty years and it shows. Didn't want this fight. Is going to finish it anyway.

fields.name: Blues Officer
fields.description: Cahill's people. Doesn't say anything incriminating. Doesn't need to â€” the badge does the threatening. Trained, structured, and operating with the full weight of the system behind him. Combat with a Blues officer is a serious escalation. The heat cost is significant. Not a casual encounter.

fields.name: Cahill's Detail
fields.description: Two plainclothes officers. Professional. Arrives without announcement. The fact that Cahill sent a detail means he's paying attention, which is the second worst thing that can happen. The worst thing is what the detail does when it gets there.

fields.name: Big Mal
fields.description: Independent heavy. For hire. No faction loyalty â€” works for whoever's paying, which tells you everything about the person who sent him. Shows up when someone wants to send a message they're paying for. The message is that they're serious enough to pay for Mal.

fields.name: The Cook
fields.description: Defensive encounter only â€” he didn't want this. Zonk cook, not a fighter. Has been improvising solutions to problems his whole life and that habit does not turn off under pressure. Better with improvised weapons than anyone expects. The workspace gives him options.
